original_question:
Isosceles triangle $\triangle ABC$ has $AB = BC.$ Let $I$ be the incenter of $\triangle ABC.$ The perimeters of $\triangle ABC$ and $\triangle AIC$ are in the ratio $125:6,$ and all the sides of both triangles have integer lengths. Find the minimum possible value of $AB.$

Present the answer in LaTex format: \boxed{Your answer}

similar_questions.question: Isosceles triangle $\triangle DEF$ has $DE = EF.$ Let $J$ be the incenter of $\triangle DEF.$ The perimeters of $\triangle DEF$ and $\triangle DJF$ are in the ratio $150:7,$ and all the sides of both triangles have integer lengths. Find the minimum possible value of $DE.$

Isosceles triangle $\triangle GHI$ has $GH = HI.$ Let $K$ be the incenter of $\triangle GHI.$ The perimeters of $\triangle GHI$ and $\triangle GJK$ are in the ratio $100:5,$ and all the sides of both triangles have integer lengths. Find the minimum possible value of $GH.$

Isosceles triangle $\triangle JKL$ has $JK = KL.$ Let $M$ be the incenter of $\triangle JKL.$ The perimeters of $\triangle JKL$ and $\triangle JML$ are in the ratio $175:8,$ and all the sides of both triangles have integer lengths. Find the minimum possible value of $JK.$

Isosceles triangle $\triangle NOP$ has $NO = OP.$ Let $Q$ be the incenter of $\triangle NOP.$ The perimeters of $\triangle NOP$ and $\triangle NQP$ are in the ratio $200:9,$ and all the sides of both triangles have integer lengths. Find the minimum possible value of $NO.$

Isosceles triangle $\triangle RST$ has $RS = ST.$ Let $U$ be the incenter of $\triangle RST.$ The perimeters of $\triangle RST$ and $\triangle RUS$ are in the ratio $125:4,$ and all the sides of both triangles have integer lengths. Find the minimum possible value of $RS.$
